title: erigeron philippii var. coxii
intro: Erigeron philippii var. coxii, also known as the Cox's Fleabane, is a perennial herb in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its essential oil and flavonoids. The health benefits of Erigeron philippii var. coxii include soothing skin irritations, wound healing, and reducing fever; it is also used to treat various health issues such as gastrointestinal problems, respiratory infections, and menstrual cramps. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
